summary: Design a deep multimodal emotion recognition (MER) architecture fusing speech (acoustic/prosodic features), facial expression video frames, and transcribed text for real-time mental health monitoring. The architecture will use modality-specific encoders (CNN/transformer for audio and video, pretrained LM for text) with a cross-modal attention fusion layer, followed by a temporal context aggregator and a classifier head for depression/anxiety/stress severity levels.
assumptions: The input is assumed to be a synchronized tri-modal stream (audio waveform, video frames, and transcribed text) captured during a therapy session or mobile interaction

Training data includes labeled clinical datasets (e.g., DAIC-WOZ, AVEC) with patient consent, covering depression/anxiety/stress severity labels alongside basic emotion annotations (valence, arousal, dominance)
A pretrained large language model (e.g., BERT or RoBERTa) fine-tuned on clinical mental-health text is available for the text modality
The system is designed for offline (post-session) analysis rather than strict real-time edge deployment, allowing moderate compute at inference time
